name: All About Chocolate - Cooking Tips
recipeInstructions:
Chocolate scorches easily, so always melt it over hot - not boiling water. Use a double boiler, or improvise with a bowl in a small saucepan over very gentle heat. Keep the water below simmering to prevent steam from curling up and hitting the chocolate. If steam gets into melted chocolate, it will thicken into a stiff mass. To rescue this, add 1-2 tablespoons of vegetable shortening (never butter) and stir vigorously. Alternatively, melt directly over very low heat in a heavy saucepan, watching carefully.
HOW TO MAKE CHOCOLATE CURLS
Warm the chocolate slightly with a vegetable peeler having a long narrow blade. Ensure the peeler is dry; draw it along the chocolate's smooth surface.
HOW TO GRATE CHOCOLATE
Ensure the chocolate block is cool and firm. Grate using a hand grater, cleaning it often to prevent clogging. Alternatively, cut into small pieces first before blending in a blender.
HOW TO STORE CHOCOLATE
Store chocolate in a cool, dry place at about 60°F. If it warms up, cocoa butter rises, forming a gray film called "bloom," which is harmless and returns to brown when melted. Avoid storing in the refrigerator or freezer; let it return to room temperature before use.
Origin: Farm Journal's Choice Chocolate Recipes
Posted in COOKING by Sharon Stevens 8/10/93
